data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260511T021034-1f7a3fb4/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260511T021034-1f7a3fb4/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260511T021034-1f7a3fb4/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260511T021034-1f7a3fb4/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260511T021034-1f7a3fb4/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-ca3e0d37">
    <title>feat(escalation): implement REFRAME path with reframer.md template and agent dispatch</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  The PostLadderExhaustionHook infrastructure (parent child ddx-e012af90) routes
  SpecGap and PersistentExecutionFailed failure classes to a REFRAME path, but no
  reframer prompt template and no reframer dispatch logic exist. When the hook
  fires for these classes, it must dispatch a strong-tier agent to rewrite the
  bead's description and AC, then apply those edits via the existing
  actionable_but_rewritten path in cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go has no reframer dispatch code.
  library/prompts/agents/reframer.md does not exist.
  The intake actionable_but_rewritten apply path exists but is only called
  from pre-claim intake (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1068-1088);
  it is not wired into the post-exhaustion recovery hook.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Add library/prompts/agents/reframer.md defining:
     - Input contract: original bead body (description + AC) and failure history
       (last N attempt summaries, stop conditions, review verdicts).
     - Output contract: JSON with fields description (string|null) and acceptance
       (string|null); null means "leave unchanged".
     - Role guidance: preserve governing refs, named tests, file:line refs, deps.
  2. Add reframer dispatch in cli/internal/agent/recovery_reframe.go:
     - runReframer(ctx, store, executor, beadID, failureHistory) → ReframeResult
     - Dispatches a smart+ tier agent using harness determined by ExecutorConfig.
     - Parses structured JSON output; validates it contains at least one non-null field.
     - Calls applyPreClaimIntakeRewrite() with the parsed edits.
     - On success: sets status=open, clears consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey,
       emits reframe-applied event with cost.
  3. Failure modes explicitly handled (each returns ReframeResult{failed:true}):
     - Timeout exceeded.
     - Empty output from agent.
     - Structurally-invalid JSON (cannot parse).
     - No-op edits: both description and acceptance unchanged after normalization.
  4. Hook (from ddx-e012af90) invokes runReframer for SpecGap and
     PersistentExecutionFailed classes; on success returns PostLadderExhaustionResult
     {attempted:true, succeeded:true, path:Reframe, costUSD:...}.

NON-SCOPE
  Decompose path (child bead 3). Circuit breaker (child bead 4).
  The fallback from reframe-failure to decompose is wired in child bead 4.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. library/prompts/agents/reframer.md exists with: (a) input contract section naming bead body fields and failure history format; (b) output contract section specifying {"description": string|null, "acceptance": string|null} JSON; (c) role guidance requiring preservation of governing refs, named tests, file:line refs, dep IDs.
2. cli/internal/agent/recovery_reframe.go exists and defines runReframer() dispatching a smart+ agent, parsing output, and calling applyPreClaimIntakeRewrite().
3. Reframe success: bead description/AC updated in store; status set to open; consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey cleared; event of type "reframe-applied" emitted with costUSD field.
4. Failure mode: timeout → ReframeResult{failed:true, reason:"timeout"}.
5. Failure mode: empty output → ReframeResult{failed:true, reason:"empty_output"}.
6. Failure mode: unparseable JSON → ReframeResult{failed:true, reason:"invalid_output"}.
7. Failure mode: no-op edits (description and acceptance both unchanged after normalization) → ReframeResult{failed:true, reason:"noop_edits"}.
8. PostLadderExhaustionHook (ddx-e012af90) invokes runReframer for SpecGap and PersistentExecutionFailed; returns PostLadderExhaustionResult{attempted:true, succeeded:true/false} accordingly.
9. TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersReframe passes: verifies SpecGap failure class routes to reframer and, on successful rewrite, bead returns to status=open with reframe-applied event.
10. TestReframerTimeout_CountsAsFailure passes: stub agent that exceeds timeout; verifies ReframeResult.failed=true with reason="timeout".
11. TestReframerNoOpEdits_CountsAsFailure passes: stub agent returns unchanged text; verifies ReframeResult.failed=true with reason="noop_edits".
12. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
13. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, kind:feature, spec:ADR-024, reliability</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-63155d5c" base-rev="9687a654e97cf640b87ef4cb3af1b13135c8ce79" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260511T021034-1f7a3fb4"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All 13 ACs are satisfied. Here's what was delivered:

**`library/prompts/agents/reframer.md`** — Input contract (title, description, acceptance, failure_history array), output contract (`{description: string|null, acceptance: string|null}`), role guidance requiring preservation of governing refs, named tests, file:line refs, dep IDs, and NON-SCOPE bullets.

**`cli/internal/agent/recovery_reframe.go`** — `runReframer()` dispatches a smart+ tier agent with cleared routing pins, extracts JSON output, validates for 4 failure modes (timeout / empty_output / invalid_output / noop_edits), applies edits via `applyPreClaimIntakeRewrite()`, clears `consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey`, and emits `reframe-applied` with `cost_usd`. `NewReframePostLadderExhaustionHook()` routes SpecGap and PersistentExecutionFailed to the reframer, returning `PostLadderExhaustionResult` with `attempted/succeeded/path/costUSD`.

**`cli/internal/agent/recovery_reframe_test.go`** — All 3 required tests pass: `TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersReframe`, `TestReframerTimeout_CountsAsFailure`, `TestReframerNoOpEdits_CountsAsFailure`. Full `./internal/agent/...` suite green.
